disclaimer: Recruiting status and eligibility change. These links are a dated research bridge, not an endorsement; verify the official page, read the current consent form, and ask questions before sharing DNA or health information.

studies.name: All of Us Research Program
studies.population_focus: A US-wide cohort intentionally seeking communities historically left out of health research, including racial and ethnic minorities, rural communities, people with disabilities, and LGBTQIA+ communities.
studies.recruitment_status: Public enrollment open; verify current US eligibility on the official join page.
studies.participation: Online consent and surveys; participants may choose to share electronic health records, physical measurements, biosamples for DNA analysis, and wearable data.
studies.consent_privacy: Consent is modular. Direct identifiers are withheld from researcher datasets, access is tiered and monitored, and a US Certificate of Confidentiality adds protection; genomic data still carries re-identification risk.

studies.name: Genes & Health
studies.consortium: Queen Mary University of London and partner NHS organisations
studies.population_focus: British Bangladeshi and British Pakistani communities, which remain underrepresented in genomic datasets.
studies.participation: People aged 16 or older who identify as Bangladeshi, British-Bangladeshi, Pakistani, or British-Pakistani provide consent, a saliva DNA sample, and permission to link NHS records; optional recall studies require further agreement.
studies.consent_privacy: Volunteers sign the consent themselves. Coded genetic and NHS-record data are linked for approved research, and any later recall study has a separate information and consent step.

studies.name: Our Future Health
studies.consortium: Our Future Health, a UK-registered charity
studies.population_focus: A large UK cohort designed to reflect the full diversity of the UK population.
studies.recruitment_status: Public enrollment open to adults aged 18 or older who live in the UK.
studies.participation: Digital consent and questionnaires, health-record linkage, and usually a blood sample and physical measurements at an appointment; some pathways use saliva or NHS Blood and Transplant samples.
studies.consent_privacy: Participants can review consent in their account. Approved researchers use de-identified data only inside a monitored trusted research environment, and follow-on contact requires permission.

studies.name: H3Africa
studies.consortium: Human Heredity and Health in Africa
studies.population_focus: African-led genomics, biobanking, ethics, and training across many distinct African populations and research sites.
studies.recruitment_status: No central public enrollment pathway; participation is project- and site-specific, so contact a listed local project and verify current status.
studies.participation: The consortium links 51 projects with different protocols; any sample, survey, or clinical involvement is defined by the local study rather than by H3Africa centrally.
studies.consent_privacy: H3Africa publishes informed-consent and ethics guidance, but each local project must explain its own data sharing, sample storage, community engagement, and withdrawal terms.

studies.name: GenomeAsia 100K
studies.population_focus: Reference genomes spanning South, East, North, and Southeast Asian populations.
studies.recruitment_status: No central public volunteer enrollment page; the official pathway is for scientific collaborators and cohort/sample partners.
studies.participation: Research partners propose a project and may contribute a relevant, appropriately consented cohort and associated data through a participating institution.
studies.consent_privacy: Individual-level data access requires a reviewed application and data-access agreement; use must remain within donor consent and de-identification protections.

studies.name: PAGE Study
studies.consortium: Population Architecture using Genomics and Epidemiology
studies.population_focus: Genomic and epidemiological analysis across established ancestrally diverse US cohorts and biobanks.
studies.recruitment_status: Active research consortium, but no direct public participant enrollment; PAGE works through existing cohorts and biobanks.
studies.participation: PAGE harmonizes genomic, phenotype, electronic-health-record, omics, and polygenic-score data contributed through its participating cohorts.
studies.consent_privacy: Consent and privacy terms come from the original contributing cohort or biobank; contact PAGE for research questions rather than sending personal DNA data.
